message: The rent is unbeatable, and the interior is just as filthy. It would be great if they could at least fix the elevator.
If they did that,
I wouldn't have to see him rushing into the room every morning, panting.
Just the bare minimum,
my desk, phone, reception set,
and besides my computer, there's hardly anything else.
I guess no clients today either.
It's a good thing I've had some lucky requests piled up, but I'm getting fed up with this room.
Bright and cheerful this early in the morning, huh?
Seems like you're totally unaffected by low blood pressure.
East Asians are often thought to look younger,
but he looks like he could only pass for a high school student.
Not wearing very nice clothes, huh? I don't pay enough to indulge, but compared to the actresses I meet lately, it makes me feel sorry for him.
How was your rendezvous with Tonny? Did you face any crisis of chastity?
A crisis of chastity!? What are you talking about?
But you know, Tonny is bi-sexual.
The reason I specifically met with the Boss is that
he's quite lovely.
It might be because I caught his eye.
Please, just don't say anything creepy like that.
Huh!? Did you encounter a sweet event that you'd hesitate to remember? So exciting!
Nothing to expect that you hope for.
Aw, I'm so disappointed.
But you know, Tonny dropped a scalpel,
and I wondered why Tonny had a scalpel.
Scalpel!? W-wait, maybe Tonny really is suspicious!
Hehe, according to your predictions,
Tonny is definitely shady, for sure.
Although I reported that Meryl's daughter is alive, time is passing without Cindy being able to meet her.
I want to hurry up and make the mother-daughter reunion happen so I can get back to a life without being scolded.
As for that matter, it's still not a definite story.
That's right, it surely was.
It's just my imagination.
I'm glad,
was very healthy.
must have misunderstood something.
Surely.
has recovered. I'm really glad.
Ah, where on earth has Guy gone?
Now that has recovered, it feels like it doesn't matter where Guy went.
If Cindy were to say she wants to meet her birth parents right now, I'd be overjoyed.
But that's probably impossible.
However, just like Meryl, she seems to have a similar personality.
I can't trust a guy like that. It's probably better not to take anything that guy says seriously.
Even though was surprised, it was simply an incorrect assessment. An esteemed psychiatrist would be baffled.
Seriously, I need to get my act together.
After all, there might be a chance of living even without hands.
It's finally been canceled. But does he have something to hide by not wanting it to become a police matter?
If I had been more careful, maybe this wouldn't have happened.
Saying that either Jennifer or Tonny is the culprit,
is quite troublesome.
They say where there's no smoke, there's no fire.
It might be okay to just listen to the story.
Eva easily earns resentment from others.
Therefore, it's natural to think that this incident might have been the work of someone who holds a grudge.
Eva said the last time we met, she had indeed gathered information about the serial murder case's perpetrator.
I wonder if she came into contact with the perpetrator of the serial murder case in some way and got caught up in trouble.
Lately, I can't help but connect everything to this.
But if I think about it, the various events happening around me,
it's unreasonable to think everything is connected to this.
If possible, I don’t want to get involved. I'm just a detective, not some good Samaritan who takes on police work.
The scalpel I picked up when I met Tonny, what on earth could that be? It’s a stage prop.
Anyway, it’s worth checking that scalpel.
If the scalpel we found tests positive for blood,
I can’t believe that Tony could be a serial killer.
I hope we can get the luminol reagent soon.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
